message:
🌱 metallb: Get rid of leader election for layer-2 mode (#815)

* 🌱 Add the-update-framework-tuf-: Yearly maintainer list cleanup mission

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🌱 Add metallb: Get rid of leader election for layer-2 mode mission

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "metallb-195-get-rid-of-leader-election-for-layer-2-mode",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "metallb: Get rid of leader election for layer-2 mode",
+    "description": "Get rid of leader election for layer-2 mode. Requested by 7+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current metallb deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your metallb version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n metallb-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metallb\nhelm list -n metallb-system 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working metallb installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review metallb configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant metallb configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n metallb-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metallb\nkubectl get configmap -n metallb-system -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=metallb\n```\nLayer 2 modes (ARP and NDP) requires a single owner machine for each service IP. Currently we do this by running master election and having the winner own all IPs."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Get rid of leader election for layer-2 mode",
+        "description": "[ Quoting <notifications.com> in \"[google/metallb] Consider getting r...\" ]\n>This algorithm results in a couple of properties:\n\nThis sgtm\n(also explains why I see 4 qps of config traffic w/ leader election)\n\nI also agree with allowing a split brain situation, instead of doing something \ncomplex.\n\n--\nMiek Gieben\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n metallb-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metallb\nkubectl get events -n metallb-system --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Get rid of leader election for layer-2 mode\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "[ Quoting <notifications.com> in \"[google/metallb] Consider getting r...\" ]\n>This algorithm results in a couple of properties:\n\nThis sgtm\n(also explains why I see 4 qps of config traffic w/ leader election)\n\nI also agree with allowing a split brain situation, instead of doing something \ncomplex.\n\n--\nMiek Gieben",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "metallb",
+      "sandbox",

+    },

@@ -0,0 +1,73 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "the-update-framework-tuf-1793-yearly-maintainer-list-cleanup",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "the-update-framework-tuf-: Yearly maintainer list cleanup",
+    "description": "Yearly maintainer list cleanup. Requested by 6+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check python-tuf installation",
+        "description": "Verify your python-tuf installation:\n```bash\npip show tuf\npython3 -c \"import tuf; print(tuf.__version__)\"\n```\nInstall if needed: `pip install tuf`. TUF (The Update Framework) is a Python library for secure software updates, not a Kubernetes workload."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review the issue context",
+        "description": "Teodora stepping down as maintainer got me thinking that we should clean up the maintainers list periodically:\n* Review both [docs/MAINTAINERS.txt](https://github.com/theupdateframework/python-tuf/blob/develop/docs/MAINTAINERS.txt) and the GitHub team permissions\n* Every maintainer account increases attack surface, so periodic review is important"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Yearly maintainer list cleanup",
+        "description": "This is easy to forget as:\n* There are multiple different critical services\n* Some permissions are not visible to everyone\n\nBut review is important as every maintainer account increases attack surface.\nSo let's remind ourselves once a year.\n\nThis relates to #1793.\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the fix",
+        "description": "Test that the fix is working as expected:\n```bash\npython3 -c \"import tuf; print(tuf.__version__)\"\npip show tuf\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Yearly maintainer list cleanup\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Periodic maintainer list cleanup is important as every maintainer account increases attack surface. Multiple critical services have permissions that are not visible to everyone, so annual review ensures the maintainer list stays current.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "the-update-framework-tuf-",
+      "graduated",
+      "security",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "the-update-framework-tuf-"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "beginner",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "graduated",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/theupdateframework/python-tuf/issues/1793",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/theupdateframework/python-tuf",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/theupdateframework/python-tuf/pull/1803"
+    },
+    "reactions": 6,
+    "comments": 12,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {

+      "python",
+      "pip"
+    ],

+  },
